--- title: "Experiment Matrix & Tracking" description: "Design Cartesian experiments, run them with ExperimentRunner, and report results." date: today format: html: self-contained: true embed-resources: true code-fold: true code-tools: true --- ## Introduction `ExperimentMatrix` defines a Cartesian product of parameters. `ExperimentRunner` executes them. `Reporter` formats results. This notebook shows the full experiment lifecycle, including optional WandB/MLflow tracking. ## Setup ::: {#setup .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import os import warnings warnings.filterwarnings("ignore") import numpy as np import pandas as pd from sklearn.datasets import make_classification from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split print("Experiment Matrix & Tracking Demo") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Experiment Matrix & Tracking Demo ``` ::: ::: ## Define Experiment Matrix ::: {#define-matrix .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} from feature_forge.experiment import ExperimentMatrix matrix = ( ExperimentMatrix() .datasets(["synthetic_a", "synthetic_b"]) .methods({"malmas": ["full", "no_memory"], "openfe": ["openfe"]}) .seeds([0, 1]) .models(["xgboost"]) .rounds([1, 2]) ) configs = matrix.generate() print(f"Total configurations: {len(configs)}") print("First 3 configs:") for c in configs[:3]: print(f" {c}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Total configurations: 16 First 3 configs: {'dataset': 'synthetic_a', 'method': 'malmas', 'method_config': {'malmas': ['full', 'no_memory'], 'openfe': ['openfe']}, 'seed': 0, 'model': 'xgboost', 'n_rounds': 1} {'dataset': 'synthetic_a', 'method': 'malmas', 'method_config': {'malmas': ['full', 'no_memory'], 'openfe': ['openfe']}, 'seed': 0, 'model': 'xgboost', 'n_rounds': 2} {'dataset': 'synthetic_a', 'method': 'malmas', 'method_config': {'malmas': ['full', 'no_memory'], 'openfe': ['openfe']}, 'seed': 1, 'model': 'xgboost', 'n_rounds': 1} ``` ::: ::: ## Dummy Experiment Function ::: {#dummy-experiment .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} def run_experiment(config: dict): """Dummy experiment that simulates feature engineering + CV.""" seed = config.get("seed", 42) n_rounds = config.get("n_rounds", 1) method = config.get("method", "unknown") # Simulate synthetic data X, y = make_classification(n_samples=200, n_features=5, random_state=seed) X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split( X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=seed ) # Simulate baseline and enhanced scores np.random.seed(seed) baseline = np.random.uniform(0.70, 0.78) gain = np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.06) * n_rounds if method == "malmas" else np.random.uniform(0.01, 0.03) enhanced = baseline + gain return { "baseline_auc": round(baseline, 4), "enhanced_auc": round(enhanced, 4), "gain": round(gain, 4), "n_features": np.random.randint(2, 8) * n_rounds, } ``` ::: ## Run Experiments ::: {#cell-run-experiments .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} from feature_forge.experiment import ExperimentRunner runner = ExperimentRunner(max_workers=1) results = runner.run(configs, run_experiment) results_df = pd.DataFrame(results) print(f"Completed {len(results_df)} runs") results_df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` {"run_name": "run_0_synthetic_a", "config_keys": ["dataset", "method", "method_config", "seed", "model", "n_rounds"], "event": "experiment_run_start", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.634835Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_0_synthetic_a", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.636347Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_1_synthetic_a", "config_keys": ["dataset", "method", "method_config", "seed", "model", "n_rounds"], "event": "experiment_run_start", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.636510Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_1_synthetic_a", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.637299Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_2_synthetic_a", "config_keys": ["dataset", "method", "method_config", "seed", "model", "n_rounds"], "event": "experiment_run_start", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.637442Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_2_synthetic_a", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.638239Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_3_synthetic_a", "config_keys": ["dataset", "method", "method_config", "seed", "model", "n_rounds"], "event": "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.645143Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_12_synthetic_b", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.645893Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_13_synthetic_b", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.647559Z", "span": null} {"run_name": "run_15_synthetic_b", "event": "experiment_run_complete", "level": "info", "timestamp": "2026-05-09T13:29:09.648150Z", "span": null} Completed 16 runs ``` ::: ::: {#run-experiments .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ## Reporter ::: {#reporter .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} from feature_forge.experiment import Reporter reporter = Reporter(results) print(reporter.to_markdown()[:800]) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` dataset method model seed n_rounds baseline_auc enhanced_auc \ 0 synthetic_a malmas xgboost 0.5 1.5 0.73865 0.807475 1 synthetic_a openfe xgboost 0.5 1.5 0.73865 0.763000 2 synthetic_b malmas xgboost 0.5 1.5 0.73865 0.807475 3 synthetic_b openfe xgboost 0.5 1.5 0.73865 0.763000 gain n_features 0 0.068825 6.0 1 0.024350 6.0 2 0.068825 6.0 3 0.024350 6.0 ``` ::: ::: ## Tracking with WandB ::: {#wandb-tracking .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} from feature_forge.experiment import WandBTracker try: tracker = WandBTracker( project="feature-forge-notebooks", ) tracker.init_run(run_name="notebook_demo", config={"demo": True}) tracker.log_metrics({"baseline_auc": 0.75, "gain": 0.04}) tracker.finish() print("WandB tracking successful") except Exception as exc: print(f"WandB tracking skipped: {exc}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ::: {.ansi-escaped-output} ```{=html} 
wandb: [wandb.login()] Loaded credentials for https://api.wandb.ai from /Users/minghao/.netrc.

wandb: W&B API key is configured. Use `wandb login --relogin` to force relogin

wandb: WARNING Using a boolean value for 'reinit' is deprecated. Use 'return_previous' or 'finish_previous' instead.
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` WandB tracking skipped: user is not logged in ``` ::: ::: ## Tracking with MLflow ::: {#mlflow-tracking .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} from feature_forge.experiment import MLflowTracker try: mlflow_tracker = MLflowTracker(project="feature_forge_notebooks") mlflow_tracker.init_run(run_name="notebook_demo", config={"demo": True}) mlflow_tracker.log_metrics({"baseline_auc": 0.75, "gain": 0.04}) mlflow_tracker.finish() print("MLflow tracking successful") except Exception as exc: print(f"MLflow tracking skipped: {exc}") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` MLflow tracking skipped: mlflow not installed. Run: uv pip install mlflow ``` ::: ::: ## Plot: Gain by Method and Rounds ::: {#cell-plot-gains .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} import matplotlib.pyplot as plt if not results_df.empty and "gain" in results_df.columns: pivot = results_df.groupby(["method", "n_rounds"])["gain"].mean().unstack() fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4)) pivot.plot(kind="bar", ax=ax) ax.set_title("Average Gain by Method and Rounds") ax.set_ylabel("AUC Gain") ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=0) plt.legend(title="Rounds") plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](05_experiment_matrix_files/figure-html/plot-gains-output-1.png){#plot-gains width=662 height=374} ::: ::: ## Summary - `ExperimentMatrix` builds a Cartesian product of datasets, methods, seeds, models, and rounds - `ExperimentRunner` executes configs sequentially or in parallel - `Reporter` generates markdown tables for papers/presentations - `WandBTracker` and `MLflowTracker` integrate with external experiment platforms
